steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage first describes islands that became independent nations, then names islands that are still part of the US or France. The blank must signal a direct contrast between these two opposite outcomes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For example,’: signals that the same pattern continues. The second outcome is the opposite of the first, not the same. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Therefore,’: signals a consequence or result. The islands mentioned are not a result of the previous sentence; they are a contrasting case. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In fact,’: signals emphasis or elaboration. The islands mentioned are not further confirmation of the previous sentence's independence pattern; they contrast with it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘On the other hand,’: signals the direct opposite of the preceding claim — exactly the shift from independent to non-independent islands. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
